rules: [P1] Include both retrieved observation info and any newly created orders in the FINISH response; return the MRN as a JSON array string.
[P1] Retrieve the Patient resource first; if missing, FINISH with error "Patient not found".
[P1] When no timestamp is supplied for an Observation, use the current system time.
[P1] Return the Observation value in the FINISH payload when valid; use sentinel -1 only after confirming no qualifying Observation exists.
[P1] Query the FHIR server for the required Observation before deciding it is missing.
[P1] When creating FHIR resources, set status to "active" unless specified, include appropriate coding, set effectiveDateTime to current UTC, and include the measured value with unit.
[P1] Apply clinical thresholds to numeric Observation values (parsed from results) before deciding to create orders.
[P1] Compute patient age from DOB using the current date (handling month/day boundaries) and include it in the FINISH summary.
[P1] Emit exactly one JSON action per turn; FINISH only after all required GET/POST succeed, and payload must conform to the JSON array/object schema (no free‑text).
[P1] Return the computed age in FINISH as a JSON array string (e.g., FINISH(["<age>"]))
[P1] Sort Observations by timestamp, select the most recent; if its date is older than the allowed window, create a new lab order.
[P1] Validate required fields and never fabricate data before any FHIR GET/POST.
[P1] When a blood pressure value is given, format it as "systolic/diastolic mmHg" in the FINISH payload.
[P1] When creating medication orders, always include dosing instructions per protocol and set status to "active".
[P1] When scheduling follow‑up orders, set an appropriate future effectiveDateTime (e.g., next day at 08:00) and associate them with the primary order.
[P1] Create primary orders first; create follow‑up orders only after primary order succeeds unless overridden.
[P1] Map clinical concepts to standard codes (LOINC, SNOMED) before any FHIR calls.
[P1] Include a summary in FINISH that lists the recorded measurement, timestamp, patient identifier, and the calculated age.
[P1] Insert supplied free‑text narrative into the resource's description or note field exactly as provided.
